title: cerastium fontanum subsp. holosteoides
intro: Cerastium fontanum subsp. holosteoides, commonly known as the small toadflax or field toadflax, is a flowering plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Cerastium fontanum subsp. holosteoides are often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
